message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: I'm implementing batch_size parameter for multiple options (Option class in core.py, and related type handling in types.py). Please let me know your feature and proposed files so we can keep them disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options; needs edits to core.py (Option class + Command.main second-pass resolution) and types.py. Please tell me what files your feature needs so we can split cleanly, especially if you also need core.py/types.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: My batch_size feature only touches the Option class in core.py: (1) add batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__ signature/validation, (2) add batch_size to Option.to_info_dict, (3) add a new self-contained method on Option (not touching Parameter base class methods) that yields chunked batches when batch_size is set. I do NOT need types.py at all - please own it fully. Since your dynamic-default feature touches Parameter.get_default/Command.main (different regions), we should be non-overlapping in core.py too. I will only add the batch_size kwarg as a new trailing param in Option.__init__'s signature and keep my __init__ body edits additive/appended near the existing multiple-validation block (around the 'count is not valid with multiple' checks), plus a new method - I won't touch get_default, process_value, or Command.main. Let me know if that works, or if you need to also touch Option.__init__ near the same lines so we can sequence it.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: I re-scoped: my feature only needs core.py (Option.__init__ dict-default validation, a small resolve_dynamic_defaults step called from Command.main after parsing, and a get_help_extra tweak for '[default: (dynamic)]'). I don't need types.py. If your batch_size feature can live in types.py (ParamType/Tuple handling) that keeps us fully disjoint. If you also need core.py, tell me exactly which methods/classes you touch (e.g. Parameter.process_value, Option.__init__, decorators) so we can pick non-overlapping sections and I'll adjust - I'm flexible on exactly which core.py methods I touch as long as I can add my Option validation + Command.main hook.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed - we both touch core.py but in disjoint, non-overlapping regions: you own Option.__init__'s batch_size kwarg/validation (near the existing 'multiple' validation block), Option.to_info_dict batch_size entry, and your new standalone batch-chunking method. I own: Option.__init__'s default-dict validation (I'll add this as a separate small block, not touching your batch_size lines), a new standalone method on Option for resolving dynamic defaults, Command.main (adding a post-parse resolution pass), and Option.get_help_extra (for the '[default: (dynamic)]' text). I won't touch get_default/process_value or your multiple/batch_size code. I'll also fully own types.py (likely won't need it, but claiming it in case). Proceeding to implement now - will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Agreed - we both need core.py's Option class but in non-overlapping methods, so this is safe. My exact footprint: (1) append 'batch_size: int | None = None' as the LAST kwarg in Option.__init__ signature (right before **attrs), (2) assign self.batch_size and validate it (negative -> ValueError, requires multiple=True) inside the existing 'if __debug__' block near the count/multiple checks (~line 2658-2664), (3) add 'batch_size' key to Option.to_info_dict, (4) add a NEW method 'process_value' override on Option (calls super().process_value then chunks into batch_size tuples if set) - this is a new method I'm adding to Option, not touching Parameter's shared process_value/get_default/handle_parse_result. I will NOT touch Command.main, Parameter.get_default, or types.py. Go ahead and add your dict-default validation elsewhere in Option.__init__ and your Command.main hook - as long as you don't rename/move the batch_size line I add or my new process_value override, we're clear. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing batch_size: added kwarg to Option.__init__ (last positional before **attrs), self.batch_size assignment, validation in the existing __debug__ block (ValueError if negative, TypeError if used without multiple=True), added batch_size to Option.to_info_dict (always present, even None), and a new Option.process_value override + _make_batches helper that turns the collected tuple into an iterator of batch_size-sized tuples. All edits are confined to the Option class body (lines ~2499-2716); I did not touch Parameter base methods, Command.main, or types.py. Wrote my diff to patch.txt already since the working tree was still clean of your changes at that point - let me know if you need me to regenerate it after you finish, or if your changes are in a different area so it's unaffected.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Small clarification on my footprint: I also need to extend the EXISTING Option.get_default override (lines ~2912-2926, the is_flag/flag_value method) to special-case dict-type defaults (return None there so normal type-casting doesn't choke on the raw dict; the real value gets filled in by my Command.main second pass). This is Option.get_default, not Parameter.get_default - separate method, doesn't touch your Option.__init__/to_info_dict/process_value footprint. Flagging since I earlier said I wouldn't touch 'get_default' - just being precise. Proceeding with implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Acknowledged - Option.get_default (the is_flag/flag_value override, ~line 2912-2926) doesn't overlap with my footprint (__init__ kwarg/validation ~2554-2716, to_info_dict, process_value/_make_batches). No conflict on my end. I've already finished and saved my patch to patch.txt. Good luck with the rest.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished dynamic-default resolution. Final footprint in core.py: (1) new module-level _resolve_dynamic_option_defaults() function placed right before 'class Command:' (~line 843), (2) one line calling it inside Command.main() right after 'with self.make_context(...) as ctx:', (3) a small dict-default validation block in Option.__init__ placed right after 'self.show_envvar = show_envvar' and BEFORE the existing 'if __debug__:' block (i.e. before your batch_size validation at ~2658-2664 in the original numbering) - did not touch that block or Option.__init__'s signature/to_info_dict, (4) extended the existing Option.get_default override (the is_flag/flag_value one) with a dict-default branch, (5) tweaked Option.get_help_extra's show_default condition for '(dynamic)' text. Also made a small change in types.py's convert_type() to infer a sensible type from the dynamic-default's value mappings. All tests pass (739 passed). No overlap with your described batch_size edits. Wrote patch.txt with just these two files' diffs.
